title: IEC 61511 Clause 8.2.4 SIS cybersecurity risk assessment freshness + ISA-TR84.00.09 / IEC 62443-3-2 zone-and-conduit risk-register evidence chain
exclusions: Excludes SIS instances that operate fully isolated (no communications to the BPCS, no external network, no portable-media path) AND for which the isolation is independently verified — a rare configuration; air-gap claims require independent attestation under ISA-TR84.00.09 §4.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.63.2` archived monthly to `audit_evidence` with `iec_clause="IEC-61511-Cl-8-2-4"`. Every SIS pins (sis_id, cra_last_completed_at, cra_method, cra_findings_open_count, cra_findings_highest_severity, zone_conduit_register_ref, isa_84_00_09_alignment, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: IEC 61511 Edition 2 Clause 8.2.4 was added specifically to bridge functional safety and cybersecurity. It mandates: 'a security risk assessment shall be carried out to identify the security vulnerabilities of the SIS, which... could lead to a loss of the required safety function performance'. The 2016 Edition language is mandatory. This UC operationalises the periodic-CRA cadence (typically annual or on major-change), the open-finding remediation status, and the alignment with the recognised methodologies (ISA-TR84.00.09 / IEC 62443-3-2).
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that every SIS has a current cybersecurity risk assessment with open findings actively remediated.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `SIS Cybersecurity Risk Posture` dashboard, the per-SIS Cybersecurity Risk Assessment (CRA) report, the IEC 62443-3-2 zone-and-conduit register, the ISA-TR84.00.09 methodology alignment statement.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISA-TR84.00.09 §4 (Integrated cybersecurity programme) and §5 (Cybersecurity risk assessment) provide the methodology for IEC 61511 Clause 8.2.4. Aligning with §4 + §5 — including the documented methodology, the SIS-cyber crossover team competence, the integration with IEC 62443-3-2 zone-and-conduit definition — is the standard-of-care evidence that a CRA was properly executed.
compliance.controlObjective: Confirms the CRA followed ISA-TR84.00.09 §4 + §5 methodology.

compliance.assurance_rationale: IEC 62443-3-2 (Security risk assessment for system design) is the explicit IEC-series methodology referenced by ISA-TR84.00.09 for the CRA. This UC enforces the zone-and-conduit register limb (a 62443-3-2 deliverable) but full assurance against 62443-3-2 additionally requires Security Level Target (SL-T) assignment, Capability Security Level (SL-C) verification, and Achieved Security Level (SL-A) post-implementation — those are tracked in UC-22.63.7.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides the zone-and-conduit + risk-register limb of 62443-3-2 for the SIS scope.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A SIS protecting a steam-cracker reactor has not had a CRA refreshed since January 2024 (>24 months stale), with three open critical findings unremediated for >180 days. UC-22.63.2 MUST flag with risk_score=100.
controlTest.negativeScenario: The same SIS has a CRA completed October 2025 (within last 12 months), with one open finding under a documented risk-acceptance signed by the Plant Manager and the IEC 62443-3-2 zone-and-conduit register current. UC-22.63.2 MUST record risk_score=0 (or risk_score<=30 for the open-but-accepted finding).

dataSources: KV Store `iec_61511_sis_cra_register_lookup` (sis_id, cra_id, cra_method, cra_last_completed_at, cra_assessor_ref, isa_84_00_09_alignment, iec_62443_3_2_alignment, zone_conduit_register_ref, cra_findings_open_count, cra_findings_highest_severity, cra_risk_acceptance_refs). KV Store `iec_61511_cra_findings_lookup` (finding_id, sis_id, finding_severity, finding_opened_at, remediation_due_at, remediation_closed_at, risk_acceptance_ref).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (Splunkbase 6849).
spl:
| inputlookup iec_61511_sis_cra_register_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_since_cra = round((now_ts - cra_last_completed_at) / 86400, 1)
| eval cra_stale = if(days_since_cra > 365, 1, 0)
| eval cra_very_stale = if(days_since_cra > 730, 1, 0)
| eval cra_missing = if(isnull(cra_last_completed_at), 1, 0)
| eval isa_84_aligned = if(isa_84_00_09_alignment = "compliant", 1, 0)
| eval iec_62443_3_2_aligned = if(iec_62443_3_2_alignment IN ("compliant","partial"), 1, 0)
| eval zone_conduit_present = if(isnotnull(zone_conduit_register_ref) AND zone_conduit_register_ref != "", 1, 0)
| join type=left sis_id [
    | inputlookup iec_61511_cra_findings_lookup
    | eval finding_open = if(isnull(remediation_closed_at) AND isnull(risk_acceptance_ref), 1, 0)
    | eval finding_age_days = round((now() - finding_opened_at) / 86400, 1)
    | eval critical_overdue = if(finding_open = 1 AND finding_severity IN ("critical","high") AND finding_age_days > 180, 1, 0)
    | stats sum(finding_open) as open_count, sum(critical_overdue) as critical_overdue_count, max(eval(if(finding_open = 1, finding_age_days, 0))) as oldest_open_finding_days by sis_id
  ]
| eval open_count = coalesce(open_count, 0)
| eval critical_overdue_count = coalesce(critical_overdue_count, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    cra_missing = 1, 100,
    cra_very_stale = 1, 100,
    critical_overdue_count > 0, 100,
    cra_stale = 1, 90,
    isa_84_aligned = 0, 80,
    zone_conduit_present = 0, 75,
    open_count > 5, 60,
    iec_62443_3_2_aligned = 0, 50,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +days_since_cra
| table sis_id cra_last_completed_at days_since_cra cra_method isa_84_00_09_alignment iec_62443_3_2_alignment zone_conduit_register_ref open_count critical_overdue_count risk_score
description: Reconciles every SIS in the operating envelope against three operational limbs of IEC 61511 Clause 8.2.4: (i) CRA freshness (annual cadence; >24-month-stale CRAs are presumptively non-compliant), (ii) ISA-TR84.00.09 §4 + §5 methodology alignment and IEC 62443-3-2 zone-and-conduit register presence, and (iii) open-CRA-finding remediation discipline (critical/high findings overdue >180 days). Triple-100-scoring conditions: CRA missing, CRA >24 months stale, or a critical finding overdue >180 days — any of these is a defensible regulator-finding by an OSHA / COMAH / Seveso / IRDA auditor.
value: IEC 61511 Edition 2's Clause 8.2.4 is the most consequential single-paragraph change in process-safety regulation in the last decade: it converts cybersecurity from a 'nice-to-have' to a SIS-validity prerequisite. Lloyds Register, TÜV, and exida regularly cite Clause 8.2.4 in SIS recertification audits, and post-incident investigators (US CSB, UK HSE) increasingly cite Clause 8.2.4 as the 'expected control' whose absence is contributory negligence. A stale CRA produces a four-fold exposure: (i) regulator finding, (ii) insurer-rate reset, (iii) FSA recertification refusal, (iv) personal accountability for the Functional Safety Manager.
implementation: (1) Maintain `iec_61511_sis_cra_register_lookup` from the functional-safety GRC tool with a row per SIS. (2) Maintain `iec_61511_cra_findings_lookup` per CRA finding. (3) Schedule UC-22.63.2 monthly; route `risk_score = 100` to the Functional Safety Manager + Process Safety Manager + Board Safety Committee at severity P1.
visualization: (Panel 1) SIS CRA-freshness heat map. (Panel 2) Open-findings by severity and SIS. (Panel 3) ISA-TR84.00.09 + IEC 62443-3-2 alignment status. (Panel 4) CRA cadence trend (24 months).

references.title: ISA-TR84.00.09-2017 — Cybersecurity related to the safety lifecycle
IEC 62443-3-2:2020 — Security risk assessment for system design

knownFalsePositives:
1. **CRA in active refresh**: a CRA scheduled for refresh and in active execution may briefly exceed the freshness threshold — load `cra_refresh_in_progress_until` to suppress for up to 90 days. 2. **Vendor-CRA for skid-supplied SIS**: a vendor-supplied SIS with a vendor-CRA may rely on the vendor's CRA — load `vendor_cra_evidence_ref` and require an annual customer-side validation. 3. **Documented risk-acceptance**: an open finding under a Plant Manager + Functional Safety Manager + CISO triple-sign-off risk-acceptance is not an overdue finding — load `risk_acceptance_ref` and an expiry on the acceptance.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `iec_61511_22_63_2_exceptions` with the Functional Safety Manager + CISO documented approval, an explicit sis_id scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.
equipmentModels: Functional-safety GRC (exida exSILentia / Hima ExSILent / Smart Project)
reviewer: Functional Safety Manager + Process Safety Manager + CISO + Board Safety Committee + TÜV / exida assessor
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-22.63.1 (SIS lifecycle register) deployed.
- A CRA methodology aligned to ISA-TR84.00.09 §4 + §5 (typically with IEC 62443-3-2 zone-and-conduit definition).
- A CRA assessor with documented competence under both IEC 61511 Clause 5.2 + IEC 62443 series.

## Step 1 - CRA register KV Store

```ini
[iec_61511_sis_cra_register_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.sis_id = string
field.cra_id = string
field.cra_method = string
field.cra_last_completed_at = time
field.cra_assessor_ref = string
field.isa_84_00_09_alignment = string
field.iec_62443_3_2_alignment = string
field.zone_conduit_register_ref = string
field.cra_findings_open_count = number
field.cra_findings_highest_severity = string
field.cra_risk_acceptance_refs = string
field.cra_refresh_in_progress_until = time
field.vendor_cra_evidence_ref = string
```

## Step 2 - CRA findings KV Store

```ini
[iec_61511_cra_findings_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.finding_id = string
field.sis_id = string
field.finding_severity = string
field.finding_opened_at = time
field.remediation_due_at = time
field.remediation_closed_at = time
field.risk_acceptance_ref = string
```

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.63.2: SIS Cybersecurity Risk Assessment]
cron_schedule = 0 7 1 * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.63.2,reg=IEC-61511,clause=IEC-61511-Cl-8-2-4
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 - Functional-safety-specific notes

- **Major-change trigger**: a CRA must also be refreshed on any 'significant change' to the SIS or its environment — link to UC-22.63.5 Management of Change tracker.
- **SIL-cyber dependency**: where a cyber-threat could degrade SIL claim, the CRA must explicitly trace through to the SIL re-calculation — store as `sil_re_evaluation_ref`.
grandmaExplanation: The Edition 2 of IEC 61511 made cyber-security an essential part of safety system design. We check every month that each safety system has had a recent cyber-risk assessment, that the assessment followed the right method, and that every important risk found is being fixed.
